case_id: None - synthetic test worlds (Santa Catalina, Solent)

pi_items.source: Verified against verifier.py - all columns are checked
verifier.py lines 119-120 and 128-129 check 'diff_pct < 0.20' for both area and resolution
export_result.sh lines 101-105 implements this formula exactly

pi_items.metadata_value: Average of lat/long resolution in meters
pi_items.verified_value: (res_lat + res_lon) / 2, where res_lat = (lat_ext * m_per_deg_lat) / height, res_lon = (lon_ext * m_per_deg_lon) / width
pi_items.source: export_result.sh lines 107-112 calculates resolution as specified

pi_items.metadata_value: Real Santa Catalina Island is at approximately 33.37°N, 118.42°W
pi_items.verified_value: Setup uses 33.20°N, 118.60°W (differs by ~0.17° in both lat and lon)
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 19-27; Real coordinates from latitude.to and latlong.net

pi_items.verified_value: Setup uses 50.70°N, 1.60°W (differs by ~0.07° in lat, ~0.27° in lon)
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 29-37; Real coordinates from Wikipedia and latlong.net

privileged_info_summary: The world_geodetic_audit task uses synthetic test worlds (Santa Catalina and Solent) with hardcoded coordinates. Critical discrepancy found: the metadata declares a coordinate tolerance of 0.02°, but the verifier.py actually implements a tolerance of 0.05°. All CSV column requirements are verified to be correct. Area calculation uses (LatExtent*60) * (LongExtent*60*cos(mid_latitude)) formula, and resolution is the average of latitude and longitude resolutions. The task requires CSV output with all 10 specified columns and a text report with minimum 50 lines, technical terminology, and training recommendations.
